name: Electron anomalous magnetic moment (cleanest QED test)
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the measured electron anomalous magnetic moment a_e = (g_e - 2)/2 to its experimental precision of ~13 significant figures. The Northwestern 2023 measurement gives a_e = 1.15965218059(13) x 10^-3, the most precisely measured fundamental quantity in physics.

regime.description: Electron Compton wavelength; QED at energies <<EWSB scale.

current_bound.reference: Fan, Myers, Sukra, Gabrielse, 'Measurement of the Electron Magnetic Moment', PRL 130, 071801 (2023), arXiv:2209.13084

what_it_excludes: Any framework modifying QED below the electroweak scale at relative precision better than ~10^-10 fails this test. The current ~2.4 sigma discrepancy between SM predictions using alpha measured by Cs-recoil interferometry vs Rb-recoil interferometry is a tension in the determination of alpha, NOT in QED itself, but any candidate framework affecting either QED or alpha-determining atomic physics has to address it.

parameterization.name: SMEFT dipole operator C_egamma at electron flavor index, plus the structure of QED at five-loop accuracy. The SM calculation uses alpha from Cs or Rb atom interferometry as input.
parameterization.reference: Aoyama, Kinoshita, Nio, 'Theory of the Anomalous Magnetic Moment of the Electron', Atoms 7, 28 (2019); Morel et al., Nature 588, 61 (2020) (Rb alpha); Parker et al., Science 360, 191 (2018) (Cs alpha).
parameterization.summary: Any deviation enters as Im(C_egamma^11) / Lambda^2 (BSM dipole) plus uncertainty in alpha that propagates into a_e^SM.

procedural.predicted_observable: a_e = a_e^QED(alpha) + a_e^EW + a_e^had + delta a_e^BSM, with delta a_e^BSM = (m_e v / sqrt(2)) Re(C_egamma^11) / Lambda^2.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |delta a_e^BSM| < ~3 x 10^-13 (from experimental precision plus theory uncertainty including alpha).
framework_inputs: Predicted modification to the electron magnetic dipole, in SMEFT or directly. If the framework affects atomic physics relevant to alpha-determination (Cs or Rb recoil), include that contribution.

primary_references.citation: Fan, Myers, Sukra, Gabrielse, 'Measurement of the Electron Magnetic Moment', PRL 130, 071801 (2023)
Aoyama, Kinoshita, Nio, 'Theory of the Anomalous Magnetic Moment of the Electron', Atoms 7, 28 (2019)
Morel et al., 'Determination of the fine-structure constant with an accuracy of 81 parts per trillion', Nature 588, 61 (2020)
